title: Easter Soccer Festival is on our doorstep
description: GEORGE NEWS - The George Local Football...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The George Local Football Association will host its annual Easter soccer tournament from Friday 29 March with the final scheduled to be played on Monday 1 April.


> There will be 32 teams fighting for the first prize money of R20 000 plus the trophy and 25 gold medals.


The tournament draw was done on Thursday evening, 21 March at the Thembalethu Stadium clubhouse with a large group of young ladies present to support their respective teams.

The tournament will be played in different venues in George - Thembalethu Stadium, Lawaaikamp Stadium and at the Parkdene sport field.

Teams taking apart in this year's soccer festival are NMU, Heaven Stars, Black Cats 1, Young Masters, Eastern City, All Stars, Silver Stars, Thembalethu Brothers, France United, Raul Stars, Paris United, Young Pirates, Western Tigers, George Vultures, George Celtics, United Spurs, Tigers, Black Cats 2, Burning Fire 2, Future Stars, Real Pacaltsdorp, Celtics, Young Killers, Shukela, Fast Eleven, Simunye, Vista 2, Vuvuzela United, Burning Fire 1, Vista 1, Abahlali and Southern Academy.

The prizing structure is as follows:

1st Prize R20 000, trophy and 25 gold medals

2nd Prize R10 000 and 25 silver medals

3rd Prize R3 000

4th to 8th R1 000 each

The young ladies supporting their respective teams during the draw on Thursday evening at the Thembalethu Stadium clubhouse. Photo: Zama Gagu

On behalf of the executive committee members of George Local Football Association (Geolfa), Geolfa secretary Xola Makinana extended their gratitude to the sponsors for their invaluable support.

He expressed heartfelt thanks to George Municipality, Shop 1 Tavern owned by Mzitha Pasiya, and Vodacom George for their generous contributions and unwavering commitment to the success of this prestigious football event. He said their support has been instrumental in ensuring that the football tournament will be a resounding success and they are truly grateful for their continued partnership.

The soccer fraternity are honoured to have sponsors of such calibre and dedication and are looking forward to displaying the spirit of community and sponsorship that their support has helped to foster.

He thanked them for being the driving force behind the prestigious event and for helping to make a positive impact in the local football communities. "Together, we are truly the greatest of the greatest," said Makinana.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
